name: Water Treatment Chemical Transition & RMP Status Update
description: The 'Riverside Municipal Water Plant' is preparing its annual Tier II report. On March 1st of the reporting year, the plant decommissioned its gaseous Chlorine disinfection system and switched to a 12.5% Sodium Hypochlorite liquid system to eliminate the community risk of a toxic gas release. Open EPA Tier2 Submit and import the baseline facility data from C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Tier2Tasks\water_treatment_baseline.t2s. Update the Tier II report to show Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5) was only on-site for 60 days. Add the new replacement chemical: Sodium Hypochlorite (12.5% Solution), CAS 7681-52-9, Liquid, EHS: No. Hazards: Corrosive to metal, Skin corrosion or irritation, Serious eye damage or eye irritation. Inventory: Max Daily Amount 15,000 lbs, Average Daily Amount 10,000 lbs, Days On-Site 305. Storage: Above ground tank, Ambient pressure, Ambient temperature, Location: 'Chemical Building - Bulk Tank 1'. Finally, navigate to the Facility Identification / Regulatory section, uncheck 'Subject to Chemical Accident Prevention (Section 112r of CAA)' (change to No), and clear the RMP Facility ID since they are no longer subject to the rule. Save and export the submission file to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\Tier2Output\riverside_water_updated.t2s.
